title: Arbel et al - AI A Corps
description: Yonathan Arbel, Simon Goldstein & Peter N. Salib — How to Count AIs: Individuation and Liability for AI Agents
show_notes:
Yonathan Arbel, Simon Goldstein & Peter N. Salib — How to Count AIs: Individuation and Liability for AI Agents

Arbel, Goldstein, and Salib address the problem of identifying and individuating AI agents for purposes of legal accountability. They propose the "Algorithmic Corporation" or "A-corp," a legal-fictional entity owned by humans but run by AIs, which solves both "thin" identity (connecting AI actions to human principals) and "thick" identity (distinguishing AI agents from one another as discrete, persistent entities).

Chapters:
2:29 Introduction
17:04 I. The Problem of AI Identity
57:57 II. The A-Corp: A Legal Solution to AI Identity
1:44:28 III. Implementation Pathways
1:57:12 IV. Objections and Responses
2:02:49 Conclusion
